pilot_limitations: N=100 caps PCA rank at 100, making all PCA projections identical (k_actual=100 for all requested k>100)
Random projection Recall@50 is noisy and non-monotonic at pilot scale (N=100, 66 ref queries)
Smoking-gun test inconclusive: PCA cannot test k=d=2048 with only 100 samples
Bootstrap CIs are wide due to small ref set (66 queries, 52 with relevant facts)

random_projection.interpretation: Peak at k=256 (k/d=0.125) with decline at larger k. Non-monotonicity likely due to pilot noise. The flat/declining pattern at k>256 is consistent with signal saturation -- adding more random dimensions captures noise rather than signal.

pca_projection.reason: N=100 < all requested k, so PCA always projects to rank 100 space
pca_projection.interpretation: PCA at rank 100 matches random projection at k=64 and k=1024, suggesting the gradient Gram matrix eigenstructure is captured. Full-scale experiment (N>>d) needed to test the smoking-gun hypothesis.

repsim_vs_trak_gap.interpretation: Even at optimal k=256, TRAK underperforms RepSim by ~21pp. This gap persists across all projection dimensions, supporting FM1 diagnosis: parameter-space methods suffer from signal dilution that dimension tuning alone cannot fix.

gradient_eigenspectrum.interpretation: Gradient covariance is highly concentrated in a low-rank subspace. Top eigenvalues decay by ~2.6x from 1st to 5th, consistent with H4 (gradient signal in low-rank subspace).

H5.evidence: Random projection saturates early (best at k=256, k/d=0.125). No improvement from k=256 to k=4096. Consistent with signal living in low-rank subspace, but non-monotonicity and pilot noise prevent definitive claim.
H5.full_scale_requirements: N >= 2048 (at least d) to properly test PCA projection at k=d and establish clear saturation curve

recommendations_for_full: Increase N to at least 2048 (=d for Pythia-1B) to enable meaningful PCA comparison
Add k=8192 and k=16384 to test k>2d region
Run multiple seeds to average out random projection noise
Consider also running on toxicity and ftrace tasks for cross-task validation
